title: Orientation Leaders | New Student Programs
meta_description: 2026 Student Orientation Coordinators Image
paragraphs: “Hi new students! I want you all to know how amazing your journey here a Central will be. Though it might not always be easy, it will all be worth it for the memories you make. Make the most of out every year here because it really does go by in a flash.”

“As you come into orientation, just come with an open mind and be yourself. There’s no pressure to have everything figured out, this is just the start, and you’re exactly where you’re supposed to be. Take the time to talk to people, ask questions, and explore what’s around you. The more you lean into the experience, the more meaningful it’ll become. We’re really happy to have you here.
“Get involved, introduce yourself, and don't be afraid to step out of your comfort zone. I hope you leave Orientation with the confidence to begin your journey at Central!”

“Lean into curiosity, ask bold questions, and don’t be afraid to get things wrong, growth comes from trying your best. Build connections and remember that you belong here just as much as anyone else.”

“At CCSU, I’ve realized that your experience can be more than just going to class. Take your time exploring what feels right for you and allow yourself to grow along the way. The more you pour into CCSU, the more it pours back into you.”
“I want every new student to be able to find a community on campus. There is always a space where you will belong at Central!”
“I want incoming students to feel welcome here at Central! Orientation is a great way on making new connections with others and creating memories with new people!”

“I choose to be an OL so I can make everyone feel invited and help new students transition at Ccsu. During my orientation, I wasn’t so sure of coming to central but the OLs really helped me feel like I belong here and influenced my choice of choosing central, so I hope to make similar positive impacts on new students.”
“Orientation is the place to meet new people and make new friends! Say hi, talk to individuals, make new friends, learn about one another, they don't bite!”
“My goal is for new students to feel comfortable and welcomed as soon as Orientation begins. Don’t be afraid to jump in, meet new friends, and enjoy the experience!”
“I want new students to feel as welcome as I did during my orientation. It’s a great place to meet new people and make friends.”

“Starting at a new school may feel uncomfortable, but don't let that stop you from getting out of your comfort zone! Take Orientation as a chance to meet people, ask questions, and find your place as a Blue Devil. You belong here as a member of the Central Family!!!”

“Orientation is your first steps into college. It’s where you meet new people, try new things, and start figuring out what your time at Central is going to look like. Get involved, have fun, and don’t stress it. Before you know it, you’ll feel right at home in the Blue Devil family.”

“Come as you are, embrace opportunities, and be open to new experiences! Your experience at CCSU is what you make of it, so make it an amazing one! We are so excited for you to join the Blue Devil family.”
“Orientation brings many opportunities for new incoming students; I look forward to making everyone feel welcomed and a part of the Central Family!!”

“I hope every new student I cross paths with at Orientation learns to make the most of themselves and their experiences at Central. You are your OWN person, and you can make any decision that you know will make you happy and full of life. Your story is YOURS!”

“Starting at a new school can feel overwhelming but Orientation is there to make it easier. It’s a time to meet people learn your way around and begin feeling comfortable in a new environment. Take advantage of the experience get involved and make it a great start to your journey.”

“Coming to Central means creating friendships, bonds, becoming part of the Central family. Orientation will encourage you to create those bonds and help you through your journey as you become a Central Blue Devil.”
“Remember that everyone around you is figuring things out, too. Be open to meeting new people. Ask questions when you need help and take advantage of every opportunity to get involved.”
“College is better with friends. Coming to orientation, you can meet a lot of people and create new and better connections. Come to orientation, it’s going to fun!!!”
“Central is about community and connections. There is plenty of opportunities to meet new people, join clubs, and be involved in campus activities. Welcome to the Blue Devil family!”
“Be yourself, stay open to new experiences, and don’t hesitate to make connections. Orientation is just the start of your Central journey and enjoy every moment!”
“I want the new students to feel the love and positivity central has to offer as well as feel part of the central family. Come to orientation with an open mind, I promise you’ll have fun!”

“Get involved, try new things, and don’t be afraid to take up space on this campus! This is your home away from home, and your potential here is unlimited. Orientation is here to help you find your path and thrive.”

“CCSU is your chance to be you! This is your time to meet new people, learn new things, and make memories that you will cherish forever! Embrace your experiences along the way and be brave in trying new things! I’m so excited to welcome you to Central and help you navigate your new home! Welcome new Blue Devils! 💙
“Incoming students should have a warm welcoming coming to central. Orientation is the place to truly get that blue devil feeling”

“Excited for another great year of orientation and to meet the incoming students, CCSU is a great experience and I can't wait to showcase and share all that this school has to offer to all the new students.”

“Don’t stress about having everything figured out right away. Put yourself out there, try new things, and talk to people. Have fun with orientation and stay open, you’ll start finding your people faster than you think!”
“Take advantage of your time here at Central!! Try new things and meet new people, even if it feels scary at first. These moments and memories are ones that you’ll cherish for a lifetime!”

"Welcome, New Students! I encourage you to explore everything that Central has to offer and step outside of your comfort zones. There are tons of opportunities here waiting for you, and as long as you’re taking steps towards new experiences, success will follow!"
“Never be afraid to try new things! Find things that work best for YOU and make the most out of the opportunities you have!”

“Central is a great place! When it comes to community, this is the place that you want to be. When you see an OL during the year, know that we are here for you to help you! We've been there, and we will help you through it.”
“I want new students to be excited about becoming a student here at Central, similar to how my OL’s made me feel! I am really excited to meet new students and create connections at all Orientations!”
“Every student should see CCSU as their second home, and I want to make sure the new students feel that energy and love. First impressions matter, so I hope to make your Orientation a fun one!”
“Get involved, make connections, and have fun with it! The friendships and memories you start building now can last your entire college career.”
“Don't be afraid to step out of your comfort zone, everyone is in the same boat as you! Orientation is the perfect time to make new connections, take advantage of it and enjoy your experience!!”
“Bring your all and don't be afraid to be yourself! It may be scary at first but I promise you it gets better as time goes on. Release your inner Beyonce!"

“Hey, new blue devils, welcome to CCSU! If you want to feel more at home, I recommend joining clubs and attending as many events as possible, as there you can find like-minded people and make new connections. Also, make sure to stay organized with your classes, as this will be a huge lifesaver when it comes to taking tests and exams.”

“My goal is to make sure every new student feels welcomed and supported as soon as they enter Central. I want to create an environment that provides safety as well as fun! Orientation is just the beginning of your wonderful journey at Central!”

“As a new student, your first year at Central should feel like home. There are many opportunities for you to grow throughout campus. Don't be afraid to try new things, meet new lifelong friends, join a few clubs, and most importantly, have fun along the way!”
“Give yourself grace as you start this new chapter. You don’t have to have everything figured out. Just stay open, get involved, and trust that you are capable of more than you think.”

“I hope every new student feels safe, supported, and at home as soon as they arrive. Orientation is your opportunity to meet new friends, create fun memories, and start this new chapter with excitement.”

title: Central P.A.S.S. Program | The Learning Center
meta_description: Central Connecticut State University’s PASS Program supports students of color on academic probation with guidance, peer support, and success strategies.

headings.text: If you are ready to commit to your college success, the PASS program may be right for you!
How do I engage with the PASS Program?
How do I enroll in the PASS Program?

paragraphs: PASS grant funded program, supported by the Minority Advancement Program administered by the Office of Higher Education. The program supports students of color who are on academic probation to achieve academic good standing and provides the tools needed to be successful at Central.
As a PASS student (ID 102), you would be enrolled in a one-credit course, that meets twice a week for one hour.
Provides the opportunity to identify and overcome personal barriers with the support of the PASS coordinator and your peers.
Class discussions centered on learning strategies and skills to navigating college.
If you are interested in participating in the PASS Program contact our PASS Coordinator:
Students on academic probation, are required to participate in an academic intervention.
The PASS program would serve as your academic intervention for the semester.
This is the only intervention that you receive credit for participating in.

A Resource Center- qualifying PASS participants will have the privilege of accessing textbooks for their classes, laptops, headsets, calculators, throughout the semester while participating in the program.
Presentations/ workshops with various offices on campus (financial aid, counseling, wellness, career development, financial literacy, tutoring, the writing center).
A positive and supportive environment to navigate challenges and build community.

Enroll in ID 102 - Master Student: This is a class for first time PASS program participants. Class meets twice a week in person throughout the semester. Students receive one credit for participating as well as additional benefits. The PASS program supports students of color who are on Academic Probation.

Enroll in TLC 008 – PASS: Returning PASS students are enrolled in TLC 008. When enrolled in this intervention, students participate in one-to-one meetings with the PASS coordinator. The PASS program supports students of color who are on Academic Probation.

title: Making It Possible | Central
meta_description: It's never too late to pursue your dreams.Are you considering a new degree? Do you want to gain some credentials or perhaps finish a degree program that you previously started? If so, you are not alone and Central is making it possible to further your education where you want it, when you want it, and how you want it!

headings.text: It's never too late to pursue your dreams.
Looking to complete a degree you previously started?
Have you acquired educational experience outside the classroom?
Did you start your higher education journey at another institution?
Is your current GPA keeping you from returning and finishing your degree?
Are financial concerns holding you back from completing your degree?
Do you need help balancing family obligations and your education?
Considering a new degree or gaining some credentials?
A Degree to fit all your Needs

paragraphs: Are you considering a new degree? Do you want to gain some credentials or perhaps finish a degree program that you previously started? If so, you are not alone and Central is making it possible to further your education where you want it, when you want it, and how you want it!
No matter where you are in your academic career, Central provides the support and resources necessary to achieve your educational goals.
You may be able to count past professional development to earn credit toward your degree.
Credits earned during your time at another school can be transferred to Central!
Fill out this form to be contacted by your personal Central liaison that will help you find the best path to completing your degree!
Central is proud to offer affordable, high-quality, flexible educational programs designed to meet your needs. We provide a variety of learning opportunities:
Looking to earn your bachelor's degree? Attend classes online, on-ground, or a combination of the two.

The BGS program is a flexible, educational pathway for adult learners or military veterans, transfer students, and current or past Central students who could not complete their initial degree program as planned.
Looking to learn a new skill, boost your resume, or stay current in your industry? Central offers a variety of undergraduate and graduate stackable certificates and non-credit-bearing certificates.
Central offers a variety of graduate programs that can be completed entirely online!
To maximize your potential for success, we offer a full slate of services and amenities that are open for extended hours throughout the week.

The Office of Career & Professional Development offers employment support before and after graduation. Through its employer network, the office also helps students find out who is hiring and when they are looking to hire.
The C.J. Huang Recreation Center is a workout and wellness facility, featuring fitness studios, a cardio room, a suspended indoor track, and a weight room.
The Drop-In Child Care Center provides free high-quality and developmentally appropriate educational experiences for children ages 3 to 12 to all enrolled Central students.
The Financial Aid Office is here to ensure your college education is an affordable investment in yourself. Financial aid is available for full- and part-time students.
The Learning Center provides academic coaching; tutoring; study skill workshops; placement testing; and other learning opportunities to help students sharpen and learn other skills.

The Office of Veterans Affairs offers support and services to veterans, service members, and their dependents as they transition from military to civilian campus culture. We also will help you take full advantage of the VA educational benefits available to you.
Office of Accessibility Services works to ensure the Central campus is accessible to all students. We offer assistive technologies and locations; workshops; Software, tools, and other resources.
The Student Wellness Center: Health Services provides routine medical care to full- and part-time students.
The Student Wellness Center: Counseling Services offers mental health counseling. We provide a range of services designed to support academic success and help students resolve various issues.
The Writing Center offers free, one-on-one tutoring to the Central community. In our sessions, we provide resources, strategies, and techniques for writing across disciplines and genres.
Explore over 75 graduate degrees and certificates to fill today’s job market demands.
Foster a spirit of cross-cultural understanding and provide opportunities to learn and respect the customs and values of others.
Welcome to Central, find out what’s next in your graduate school journey!
Have fun, try something new, make lifelong friends, become a leader, grow, and experience life.

“The decision to enroll in college was complicated and personal. But I considered the long-term benefits of personal growth, career happiness, and economic security that would help me overcome these challenges and achieve my goals.”

title: Success Matters - For Students | The Learning Center
meta_description: The Learning Center at Central Connecticut State University explains what to expect after receiving a Success Matters email and how to access academic support.

headings.text: I just received a Success Matters email. What happens next?
What happens at a success matters meeting?

paragraphs: If you have received an email from Central Student Success, the best thing to do is open it and review the important details of the message. Your email subject will indicate if you have received a concern or a kudos. If your email is based on an academic concern the email will indicate who has shared this concern, what class they are concerned about, and a link to register for an appointment.

Success matters meetings can take place in-person or virtually based on your preference and availability. Meetings are about 30 minutes, and are used to discuss the concern submitted, and additional concerns you may have regarding your academic success and goals at Central. Our role in The Learning Center is to provide support and the resources and connections that are best suited to the current concerns.

title: Academic Integrity Workshops | The Learning Center
meta_description: Learn about Academic Integrity Workshops at Central Connecticut State University. Support student understanding of ethical conduct and plagiarism.
paragraphs: TLC offers specialized Academic Integrity Workshops designed to promote a better understanding of academic honesty and its importance in maintaining educational standards. Faculty members may refer students to these workshops for additional guidance on academic integrity or require attendance as part of the intervention process for students suspected of academic misconduct. These workshops provide a structured environment for students to learn about proper citation practices, the consequences of plagiarism, and strategies for maintaining ethical academic standards. Typically, each session runs between 30 minutes to an hour, offering students a concise yet thorough exploration of key principles related to academic integrity.
For more information on referring students to one of our Academic Integrity Workshops, visit Central’s Office of Student Rights & Responsibilities website:

title: Lactation Room Locations | The Ruthe Boyea Women's Center
meta_description: In order to better support our students, staff, and faculty who are new mothers and wish to breast-feed/pump, Central Connecticut State University is pleased to provide private and comfortable lactation rooms located in:    Applied Innovation Hub(AIH), Room 114    Barnard Hall, Room 210    Ebenezer Bassett Hall, Room 110    Mid-Campus, Room 116-02
paragraphs: In order to better support our students, staff, and faculty who are new mothers and wish to breast-feed/pump, Central Connecticut State University is pleased to provide private and comfortable lactation rooms located in:

Breastfeeding, the key to good health of mothers and babies, also has benefits for families, employers, and the community in general. For these reasons, the Connecticut Department of Public Health, the Connecticut WIC Program and the American Academy of Pediatrics recommend feeding your baby only breast milk until age 6 months, and breast milk plus solid food from 6-12 months and beyond.

CDC's Division of Nutrition, Physical Activity, and Obesity (DNPAO) is committed to increasing breastfeeding rates throughout the United States and to promoting and supporting optimal breastfeeding practices toward the ultimate goal of improving the public's health.

Our Mission is to help mothers worldwide to breastfeed through mother-to-mother support, encouragement, information, and education, and to promote a better understanding of breastfeeding as an important element in the healthy development of the baby and mother.

Expressing Breastmilk on the Job. Readjusting to your previous work routine can be challenging enough without factoring in a breastfeeding schedule. Fortunately, you have taken the time to prepare your baby, your caregiver, your co-workers, and yourself for this new situation, so you are making the transition in the best possible way.

paragraphs: Take a deep breath! Being on academic probation can be overwhelming. The Learning Center is here to help you get the skills and support needed to raise your GPA.

Any student in their first semester of academic probation is required to complete a mandatory academic intervention provided by The Learning Center. paragraphs: New Student Programsserves as a resource for the parents and families who support our new students in their transition to college life. We know that joining a new college community may be overwhelming - NSP is here to help!

Upon confirming their acceptance, your new student will begin receiving emails from Central regarding orientation,Undergraduate Advising, placement exams, and more. Understanding what to expect with the transition to college life if critical to their success, and ensuring that they read their email is the best way for them to stay in-the-know. We ask that they begin checking their email routinely (even daily)! This habit will be a good start to becoming an autonomous and responsible member of the Central community.

Orientation programs are for newfirst year students, andnew transfer students, as well as new international students. You may check each of those tabs on this page for more information about our Orientation dates this summer.

During Summer First Year Orientation, Central hosts a family track with various sessions and workshops. Students and families begin the day together at 9 a.m., with a resource fair and welcoming remarks from out University President, Dr. Zulma Toro. After the welcome, students and families will follow separate tracks. Families will learn about various resources and programs, as well as what to expect throughout their first year at Central.

Before attending our in-person Orientation, we invite families and students to view our Pre-Orientation Online Modules atCCSU.GOorientation.com. You may use the Guest Log-In to access the program, but your student can log in with their Central bluenet ID. This engaging and interactive platform provides a direct connection to important resources and links to prepare you and your student for all you need to know before coming to campus for Orientation. Major resources within our online modules include Academics & Advising, Support Services, Policies and Safety, Paying for College, and Campus Life.
You may also access important information within Central's Blanket of Supporthere.

title: Knack | The Learning Center
meta_description: Get free, course-specific peer tutoring through Knack at Central Connecticut State University. Book sessions online or in person with successful students.

headings.text: If you have general questions about Knack and our work here at Central, please contact:

paragraphs: Central has proudly partnered with Knack — a platform enabling Central students to book course-specific peer tutoring. Knack Tutors are fellow students who have previously been successful the courses they offer assistance in; tutoring is completely free and sessions can take place in person or online. To view available tutors, visitccsu.joinknack.comand sign in with your student account.
Students are eligible to tutor for Knack if they have received a B+ or higher in a class. Additionally, a professor can recommend you to Knack.Recommendations come with additional Knack badging.
If you are experiencing issues with the platform, please reach out tosupport@joinknack.com.

title: Global Engagement at Central | Central
meta_description: Global Engagement at Central is expanding its services and programming through Study Away, Study Abroad, and International Student Support Services. These opportunities not only broaden students’ cultural horizons, but also foster global citizenship and academic collaboration, preparing students to thrive in an increasingly interconnected world. By expanding resources and support, Global Engagement at Central aims to enrich the academic experience for all while enhancing the cultural vibrancy of Central’s campus.
paragraphs: Global Engagement at Central is expanding its services and programming through Study Away, Study Abroad, and International Student Support Services. These opportunities not only broaden students’ cultural horizons, but also foster global citizenship and academic collaboration, preparing students to thrive in an increasingly interconnected world. By expanding resources and support, Global Engagement at Central aims to enrich the academic experience for all while enhancing the cultural vibrancy of Central’s campus.

The SA unit assists Central students who wish to study and live abroad to gain valuable educational and first-hand experiences with foreign languages and cultures. SA includes three categories of programs: “Semester” (to year-long) programs, “Summer” programs, and “Courses Abroad” programs. The Semester (to a yearlong) programs are available to students through a network of university partnerships around the world. “Courses Abroad” are intense, credit-bearing University classes taught by faculty, include courses at the undergraduate and graduate level, and offer for credit in many majors and in all regions of the world.

Central’s commitment to diversity would be most incomplete without the presence of international students and scholars on campus. The presence of international students and scholars on the Central campus enriches the educational environment for all. ISSS advises and supports international scholars, exchange students, degree-seeking students, as well as students who come to Central to learn English in the Intensive English Language Program (IELP). ISSS is also instrumental in creating a supportive environment that attracts and retains international students and scholars—an important component in Central’s commitment to diversity.

The mission of the Intensive English Language Program (IELP) is to provide English as a Second Language (ESL) instruction in American English and an introduction to American culture. IELP serves international students, non-native speakers of English residing in the U.S., faculty members from abroad, and foreign professionals. Specifically, IELP offers English for Academic Purposes (EAP) to individuals who are seeking enrollment into American universities and facilitates the transition of these students into mainstream academic courses.

title: Certificate Programs | Making It Possible
meta_description: CCSU's certificate programs offer high-quality educational experiences in for-credit and non-credit subjects identified through close monitoring of market demands.
paragraphs: CCSU's certificate programs offer high-quality educational experiences in for-credit and non-credit subjects identified through close monitoring of market demands.

Stackable credentials provide our students with high-quality educational opportunities, combined with the flexibility they demand in a fast-paced and rapidly changing world. Students can enroll in a short-term, focused certificate program that allows for rapid completion and career advancement while simultaneously working to complete an undergraduate or graduate degree from Central. This opportunity allows students to combine courses in new and personalized ways that build the applied skills needed to advance in their chosen field while working toward completion of a traditional degree.
Undergraduate and graduate for-credit certificate programs may be applied to a future degree and can be earned in:

Professional Education at Central uses the expertise of award-winning faculty, alumni and business experts to offer a wide variety of non-credit classes, programs, and professional certificates designed to help professionals advance in their careers and improve their knowledge. Our cutting-edge programs are intentionally designed to be offered online, on-ground, hybrid (the best of both), and HyFlex to help you balance your educational needs and the many competing demands of your life and business.

As an additional bonus, many of our non-credit certificates that can be used toward a Central degree through the American Council for Education’s articulation process! A selection of current offerings can be found below:

title: Women, Gender, and Sexuality Studies, Minor | Central
meta_description: Women, Gender, and Sexuality Studies is an interdisciplinary field of study that focuses on the experiences of women, men, and non-binary people in society and history.

headings.text: Women, Gender, and Sexuality Studies, Minor

paragraphs: Women, Gender, and Sexuality Studies is an interdisciplinary field of study that focuses on the experiences of women, men, and non-binary people in society and history.

Initially conceived as a method to include women’s contributions to knowledge and culture into education, it has evolved into a mature field that draws on feminist theories and scholarship in order to analyze how systems of gender, race and intersectionality have shaped experiences in areas such as art, literature, history, the family, science, and other dimensions of daily life. As the mission statement of the National Women’s Studies Association indicates, Women, Gender, and Sexuality Studies has as its vision a world free of sexism, misogyny, homophobia, transphobia, and racism.

At Central, you may choose to minor in Women, Gender, and Sexuality Studies. We offer courses that draw on the resources of many different departments, and we also offer three “stand alone” classes. When you take a course in Women, Gender, and Sexuality Studies, you will study with some of the most dynamic teachers on the Central campus.

While not all majors at Central require minors, individual WGSS courses can be taken in General Education study areas I, II, and III, and WGSS can be added as a minor in majors where none is required.
Browse our course listings and check out what our faculty are doing. We also periodically sponsor lectures and events (especially in March, National Women’s History Month).

The WGSS courses prepare students for various employment and professional opportunities. The minor does not prepare students for employment in one particular job, but for many different types of employment that emphasize or require strong writing skills, analytical and critical thinking, research skills, reading comprehension, and oral communication, a WGSS minor can enhance one’s credentials. Our courses and our minor complement any major degree in which students anticipate working in diverse environments and/or in which they serve the public. The Women, Gender, and Sexuality Studies minor is particularly useful for those who will major in any liberal or fine arts field, business fields, Criminology and Criminal Justice, Computer Science, Engineering, Journalism, Nursing, Sociology, Social Work, Communication, Psychological Sciences, Education, and many STEM fields.

Women, Gender, and Sexuality Studies creates a community of learners dedicated to scholarship that emphasizes feminist knowledge of ideas, the accomplishments of women and sexual minorities, and social advocacy. The minor prepares students to be thoughtful, responsible and compassionate citizens of the world. Our interdisciplinary focus allows us to offer a broad perspective on contemporary cultural problems that can be studied in depth.
This is an unofficial description for this program. For official information check theAcademic Catalog.

Women, gender, and sexuality studies is an interdisciplinary program concerned with issues that affect people due to their gender or sexual identity. The minor allows students to take courses in various departments that will enrich their lives as they explore topics such as the meanings of femininity and masculinity, sexual orientation, gender norms in society, sex, feminism, and global gender issues.

At least nine credits must be at the 300-400 level, and no more than nine credits can be from any one discipline. Special topic courses may also be used pending approval of the appropriate department chair and the Women, Gender, and Sexuality Studies coordinator.

At least nine credits must be at the 300-400 level, and no more than nine credits can be from any one discipline. Special topic courses may also be used pending approval of the appropriate department chair and the Women, Gender, and Sexuality Studies coordinator.

title: The Ruthe Boyea Women's Center | Central
meta_description: MissionThe Ruthe Boyea Women's Center exists to provide resources, to advocate, to inform, and to support personal development. The Center offers a variety of services for and about women. We sponsor educational and cultural programs designed to promote gender equity, knowledge of women's rights issues, leadership, and independence. We encourage understanding and cooperation among women of varied socio-economic groups, cultures, ethnic backgrounds, races and sexual orientations. We welcome all women and men who enter our doors.
paragraphs: The Ruthe Boyea Women's Center exists to provide resources, to advocate, to inform, and to support personal development. The Center offers a variety of services for and about women. We sponsor educational and cultural programs designed to promote gender equity, knowledge of women's rights issues, leadership, and independence. We encourage understanding and cooperation among women of varied socio-economic groups, cultures, ethnic backgrounds, races and sexual orientations. We welcome all women and men who enter our doors.

The Center is open to all of Central's community, men and women. The Women's Center values and celebrates the multiplicity of women's lives; recognizes the intersections of gender, race, sexual orientation, economic status, and other significant aspects of individual and cultural identity; accepts responsibility for opposing injustice; and commits itself to service to the University and larger communities. Feel free to stop by to see what's going on, or just hang out with the crew. Also, please check out our newsletter, and send in your letters to be published.

Women of all backgrounds can drop in and help one another grow towards personal effectiveness and independence. We encourage understanding and coming together of women of varied cultures, races and ethnicities, as well as different sexual orientations, socio-economic groups and ages. Our Center is for and about women so that both women and men are welcome to drop in and use our resources, attend activities or just hang out.

title: Age-Friendly University | Central
meta_description: In May of 2017, Central Connecticut State University became the first university in Connecticut to join the Age-Friendly University (AFU) Global Network. We are proud to join a growing number of institutions across the globe in an international effort led by Dublin City University to identify the distinctive contributions institutions of higher education can make in responding to the interests and needs of an aging population.
paragraphs: In May of 2017, Central Connecticut State University became the first university in Connecticut to join theAge-Friendly University (AFU) Global Network. We are proud to join a growing number of institutions across the globe in an international effort led by Dublin City University to identify the distinctive contributions institutions of higher education can make in responding to the interests and needs of an aging population.

Connecticut has the seventh highest median age in the United States, and by 2030 it is expected that 25 percent of the state’s population will be over the age of 60. With more people experiencing increased longevity, Central is committed to meeting the educational needs and interests of this growing group through the development of new opportunities and innovative practices in teaching, research, and community engagement. TheAFU principlesserve as a valuable guiding framework for distinguishing and evaluating how Central can shape age-friendly programs and practices, as well as identify gaps and opportunities for growth in the years to come.
For more information about the Age-Friendly University Initiative, visitDCU Age-Friendly UniversityandGSA's Academy for Gerontology in Higher Education AFU Global Network.

Having older adults in a classroom has really been eye opening for me as a student at Central because while I was in undergrad at a different university, I only had one experience in being in a class with an older adult and she ended up leaving the course shortly after it started. I love being able to hear the experiences and ideas of the older students in our classes as I find what they have to say enlightening, especially in this course specifically. Through age-friendly universities, students of all ages are able to continuously challenge ageist stereotypes and initiate intergenerational learning as well as support inclusion and diversity for the campus environment. --G.Z. (current Central student)

Scholars for Life Speaker Series- Central faculty and guests bring their passions and knowledge to the Scholars for Life Speaker series offered through Central’s Office of Professional Education. During the pandemic, the series is being offered free and online.

Over 62 Tuition Waiver Form- Connecticut residents age 62 and older can go back to school tuition free. The tuition waiver can be applied to both full and part-time matriculated students (those who have been admitted to an undergraduate or graduate degree program) and non matriculated students. Some fees are still required and registration for non matriculated students is on a space-available basis and special registration dates apply. Check with theRegistrar's Officefor session/term registration dates. For those interested in earning a degree or certificate, students must follow standard admission procedures and submit the tuition waiver once accepted to their program of choice. VisitAdmissionsto learn more about our program offerings.

Ageism First Aidis an online multi-module course designed to help change the common negative misconceptions and myths about aging by replacing them with facts that should be common knowledge. The course will support factual conversations about aging and spark widespread use of positive aging-related language to help create a more age-friendly society.
Learners will need to create a new learner account and select Central Connecticut State University. The course fee is $15 for students and $20 for nonstudents.

AARP’s Disrupt Aging Classroomis a two-hour, interactive curriculum that challenges students to examine their aging perceptions and think about how the growing aging population is relevant to their personal lives and future careers.

The Disrupt Aging Classroom curriculum is free and presented by AARP volunteer facilitators, who are trained extensively on the content and effective delivery. Facilitators can present either in the classroom or virtually through a live-video presentation. The curriculum complements any course of study and can be presented across disciplines at university campuses.

Reframing Aging Initiative- Reframing aging relies on tested strategies and evidence-informed tools that have been found to reduce implicit bias against older people. Find free resources and a toolkit to help you change the conversation around aging.

Tools for Advancing Age Inclusivity in Higher Education was designed by the Gerontological Society of America (GSA) and its Academy for Gerontology in Higher Education (AGHE), with support from AARP, to provide resources to advance age inclusivity in institutions of higher education. The suite of tools can be used by faculty, students, administrators, and other campus leaders.

title: Reporting | Office of Victim Advocacy
meta_description: Filing a Report at CentralIf you have been impacted by interpersonal violence and would like to file a report with the Title IX Coordinator in the Office of Regulatory Affairs, you may do so by completing the Reporting Form for Harassment, Discrimination, and Title IX form below.Reporting Requirements for Faculty, Staff, and Administrators
paragraphs: *On every page, there is a Safety Exit button in the same location that will take you to the Central homepage in case you need to leave our site quickly. This button will NOT delete your browser history.To exit this site quickly, click the "Exit" button the side of the page. If you are concerned about someone finding out that you visited our Website, please readInternet Browsing Safety Tips.

If you have been impacted by interpersonal violence and would like to file a report with the Title IX Coordinator in the Office of Regulatory Affairs, you may do so by completing the Reporting Form for Harassment, Discrimination, and Title IX form below.

Central faculty, staff, and administrators who become aware of a sexual assault involving a member of the Central community are required to report it to the Title IX Coordinator. Reports can be submitted through the online reporting form.
For incidents that occur on campus, you can contact Central Police at (860) 832-2375. Central Police and the OVA can also provide guidance on requesting protective orders.

The OVA can assist with requesting reasonable accommodations, including adjustments to academic, housing, transportation, or work situations. If you or someone you know has experienced interpersonal violence, please know that you are not alone. Support is available both at Central and in the local community.

title: Financial Aid Office | Central
meta_description: Explore financial aid at Central Connecticut State University. Get support with FAFSA, scholarships, the Pell Promise, and resources to fund your education.

headings.text: 2026-2027 FAFSA is now available to complete
What is the Pell Promise Initiative?

paragraphs: The Office of Financial Aid provides financial aid information, support, and guidance to Central’s current and prospective students. Our Financial Aid team recognize that each student’s journey is unique and provides each student the individual support they need to plan for their future success. The result is a financial aid package and plan that fits your need!

We encourage everyone tocomplete a FAFSA. An early FAFSA opens the opportunity to Grant funds available through the University. Late applicants can still qualify for Direct Loans and Pell Grants, if eligible.
We encourage current and Spring semester students to complete the 2024-2025 FAFSA.

At Central Connecticut State University, we’re committed to supporting the academic success of our Pell Grant recipients. That’s why we created thePell Promise Initiative —a financial aid program that ensures qualifying students can attendtuition-free.
The Pell Promise Initiative is alast-dollar scholarshipthat covers the full cost oftuition and feesfor eligible students after federal and state aid is applied.
Once your federal Pell Grant and state aid are applied, Pell Promise covers any remaining tuition and fees. This meansno out-of-pocket academic costsfor qualifying students.
We’re proud to make college more affordable — and your success more achievable — with Pell Promise.
Documents may be submitted using ourOnline Document Submission tool, emailed (be sure to Password Protect private information), or faxed (860.832.3330).
Be sure to monitor your Central e-mail address or periodically check Central Pipeline for Financial Aid Offers and/or Requirements.

The Office of Financial Literacy provides students the knowledge and support they need to successfully navigate the challenges that come along with financing an education, living on their own for the first time, and paying their bills. Tambien hablamos español.

title: The Learning Center | Central
meta_description: The Learning Center at Central Connecticut State University offers tutoring, coaching, and academic support to help all students reach their full potential.

headings.text: Because everyone needs a little TLC!
What do we do at The Learning Center?
Why Do Students Come to TLC?

paragraphs: Whether you are a first year, senior, transfer, or graduate student we are committed to your academic success!
Our mission is to provide high quality, individualized academic assistance for the students of Central Connecticut State University, so that they may reach their maximum academic potential.

The Learning Center is engaged in innovative approaches to academic support that underscore our belief that every student has the potential to be independent and successful learners. The Learning Center values collaboration and works with many departments in both Academic Affairs and Student Affairs.

The Learning Center was established as a distinct department in January 1999, having previously operated under the Educational Support Services Department, which also oversaw the Educational Opportunity Program (EOP), the Connecticut Collegiate Awareness and Preparation Program (CONNCAP), and the Upward Bound Program.

Initially located in Copernicus Hall during the summer of 1999, The Learning Center transitioned from the Division of Student Affairs to the Division of Academic Affairs in August 2007. In the spring of 2011, it relocated to Willard Hall, before moving to Carroll Hall in 2016 while Willard and DiLoreto halls underwent renovations.
In the spring of 2019, The Learning Center returned to the newly renovated Willard-DiLoreto Hall, where it continues to serve students today.
The Learning Center is located in Willard-DiLoreto Hall, Room D 316
The center is equipped with spaces for tutoring, academic coaching, and placement testing.
We offer a variety of academic support services for all types of students from academic coaching to tutoring. The Learning Center currently provides the following services:

At The Learning Center, students can access a wide range of academic support services to enhance their success. Whether working with an academic coach to improve study habits, participating in peer tutoring, or learning effective test-taking strategies, students are empowered to reach their full potential. The Learning Center also offers guidance for managing time, staying motivated, and navigating the transition to college-level academics. Additionally, students can complete placement exams, fulfill academic probation interventions, and pick up tools like our Semester-At-A-Glance to plan for a successful semester.

The Learning Center is staffed by a mix of undergraduate students, graduate students, and professionals. You will have the opportunity to work with your peers who are trained tutors as well as with trained graduate student academic coaches. To learn more about who we are, check out ourstaff directoryandcoaches’ page!

title: Central and UHART announce new articulation agreement for Athletic Training students | Central
meta_description: Central Connecticut State University and the University of Hartford have teamed up to create a new, streamlined pathway for s

headings.text: Central and UHART announce new articulation agreement for Athletic Training students

paragraphs: Central Connecticut State University and the University of Hartford have teamed up to create a new, streamlined pathway for students pursuing careers in athletic training—making it easier than ever to go from undergraduate study to a graduate degree in the field.

Through a newly signed articulation agreement, qualified students in the University of Hartford’s Exercise Science program will have a direct route into Central’s Master of Science in Athletic Training (MSAT) program. The agreement guarantees admission for up to five students each year who meet established academic and program requirements, with the possibility of additional students accepted based on program capacity.

“This partnership reflects our deep commitment to creating clear pathways for all students to achieve their goals,” said Central President Dr. Zulma R. Toro. “By working together with the University of Hartford, we are making it easier for aspiring athletic trainers to move confidently from undergraduate study to professional practice, equipped with the knowledge, experience, and support they need to succeed.”

Students in the program will apply by April 1 during their senior year at the University of Hartford and, upon successful completion of their bachelor’s degree and required prerequisites, will transition directly into Central’s MSAT program for the summer session. Admission through this pathway is non-competitive for students who meet the criteria, providing a more accessible and predictable route to graduate study.

In addition to streamlined admissions, the partnership emphasizes collaboration between the two institutions, including coordinated advising, career counseling, and ongoing support to help students succeed academically and professionally.

Central’s MSAT program is accredited by the Commission on Accreditation of Athletic Training Education (CAATE) and prepares students for careers working with athletes and active populations in a variety of clinical and performance settings.
The agreement took effect on March 1, 2026, and will remain in place through February 2028, with the option to extend.

Friend of the University Award Criteria

paragraphs: The Distinguished Alumni Service Award is presented to a graduate of Central Connecticut State University (Central Connecticut State College, Teachers College of Connecticut or New Britain Normal School) in recognition of outstanding service to the University or the Alumni Association and to the community, since graduation, and for professional excellence in one's chosen profession.

The Young Alumni Service Award is presented to a graduate of Central Connecticut State University in recognition of outstanding service to the University or the Alumni Association and to the community, since graduation, and for professional excellence in one's chosen profession. The recipient shall be 40 years of age or younger.

The Community Service Award may be presented to a graduate for extraordinary community service, and for contributions that advance the improvement of society, thereby reflecting favorably on the University.

The Friend of the University Award may be presented to a non-graduate of the University who has provided distinguished leadership and/or service to Central Connecticut State University. Friend may include an individual; organization; corporation; or member of Central’s faculty or staff.
